channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KREX 5

item.title: New Social Security chief contradicts claims that millions of dead people are getting payouts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The new head of the Social Security Administration said Wednesday that deceased centenarians are “not necessarily receiving benefits," contradicting claims that tens of millions of dead people over the age of 100 are getting payments from the agency.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he will check with Trump about DOGE stimulus checks
item.description: (KRON) -- Elon Musk said he will check with President Donald Trump about the possibility of sending $8,000 "DOGE stimulus" checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: DOGE notches courtroom wins as Elon Musk crusades to slash federal government
item.description: Although some parts of President Donald Trump’s agenda are getting bogged down by litigation, Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency is having better luck in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Chris Megerian And Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tens of millions of dead people aren't getting Social Security checks, despite Trump and Musk claims
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOGE affiliate may access IRS taxpayer records: Report
item.description: An IRS employee with affiliations to the Department of Government Efficiency may be able to gain access to IRS taxpayer records.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: AP: Tens of millions of dead people aren't collecting Social Security
item.description: The Trump administration is falsely claiming that tens of millions of dead people over 100 years old are receiving Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: A comprehensive look at DOGE's firings and layoffs so far
item.description: Thousands of federal government employees have been shown the door in the first month of President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social Security chief steps down over DOGE access to recipient data: Reports
item.description: The Social Security Administration’s acting leader stepped down from her role over requests from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to access recipient data, according to several reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-Israel protest in NYC devolves into violence and mayhem
item.description: A group of anti-Israel demonstrators descended on an Orthodox Jewish neighborhood in Brooklyn on Tuesday leading to violent clashes and chaotic scenes, according to reports and video.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illinois governor to back 'screen-free schools' and join national trend to ban cellphones in class
item.description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker is aiming to improve student achievement, social interaction and the mental health of public school students by proposing a statewide ban on cellphones in classrooms, an idea that is rapidly gaining traction nationally regardless of political persuasion.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CIA uses drones to sniff out cartels and fentanyl labs in Mexico: US official
item.description: The CIA has been flying MQ9 Reaper drones over Mexico to help sniff out dangerous cartels and fentanyl laboratories, in partnership with the country's government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father-son duo survives night in Utah wilderness after finding 'miracle' backpack
item.description: In January, a 15-year-old boy was rescued from a southern Utah trail, but left his backpack behind. Weeks later, a father and son found that backpack and used it to survive.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, WGN 9

item.title: Jury finds A$AP Rocky not guilty in felony assault trial
item.description: A verdict has been reached in the felony assault trial of rapper A$AP Rocky: NOT GUILTY. Jurors announced they’d made a decision just hours after the deliberations began Tuesday morning in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Questions emerge about what may have caused Delta plane to burst into flames and flip over
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Investigators will consider the weather conditions, as well as the possibility of human error or an aircraft malfunction as they try to determine why a Delta Air Lines jet burst into flames and flipped upside down as it tried to land in Toronto, aviation experts said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: NASA reports increased chance of ‘city killer’ asteroid hitting Earth
item.description: An asteroid big enough to wipe out a city now has an increased chance of hitting Earth in seven years, NASA reported on Tuesday. NASA first discovered the 130-to-300-foot-wide asteroid, named 2024 YR4, in December 2024, finding it only had a roughly 1% chance of impacting Earth on its trajectory.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WGN 9

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NYC voters would likely support Gov. Kathy Hochul removing Adams as mayor, AP VoteCast shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As New York Gov. Kathy Hochul weighs whether to remove New York City Mayor Eric Adams from office, AP VoteCast shows that many New York City voters in last year's election were already on board with the move.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Leader of cultlike Zizians linked to 6 killings faces bail hearing in Maryland
item.description: CUMBERLAND, Md. (AP) — The leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians that is linked to six killings in three states faces a bail hearing Tuesday along with two others who were arrested with her in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man drives 730 miles to burn down ex-girlfriend's new boyfriend's house, police say
item.description: "At the time the fire started, all the residents in the house were sleeping. This is obviously a nightmare for anyone," an official said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Columbine survivor Anne Marie Hochhalter dies decades after shooting
item.description: Anne Marie Hochhalter, a survivor of the Columbine High School mass shooting, died Sunday, her former principal, Frank DeAngelis, told Nexstar's KDVR on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta jet flips upside down on a snowy Toronto runway and all 80 aboard survive
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — A Delta Air Lines jet flipped on its roof while landing Monday at Toronto’s Pearson Airport, but all 80 people on board survived and those hurt had relatively minor injuries, the airport’s chief executive said.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top Russian, US officials discuss improving ties, ending the Ukraine war, without Kyiv
item.description: RIYADH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The top diplomats from Russia and the U.S. met Tuesday in Saudi Arabia to discuss improving ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine — talks that represented a rapid and major change in American foreign policy under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, BARAA ANWER and DASHA LITVINOVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schools around the US confront anxiety over Trump's actions on immigration
item.description: In Fresno, California, social media rumors about impending immigration raids at the city's schools left some parents panicking — even though the raids were all hoaxes.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis on Monday named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier as Florida’s attorney general, handing the state’s top law enforcement role to a trusted aide who has helped orchestrate and defend some of his most divisive initiatives.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: A plane that had gone missing Friday night was found early Saturday morning in a remote, wooded area in north Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9
